frameworks.name: bounded-capacity serial bottleneck
frameworks.one_line: Decisions update one-at-a-time despite parallel sensory intake.

frameworks.name: evidence accumulation / sequential sampling
frameworks.one_line: Standard accumulator extended with buffered evidence and time-multiplexed access.

frameworks.name: parallel sensory acquisition
frameworks.one_line: Early stimulus features are encoded simultaneously across dimensions.

core_question: When two decisions concern one object, are evidence streams incorporated in parallel or serially?
key_assumptions: color and motion are separable stimulus dimensions of the same display
decision formation can be decoupled from sensory acquisition
evidence samples can be buffered while decision access is blocked
manipulations: requirement to report both decisions about a single dynamic random-dot display
key_findings: accuracy of one decision is unaffected by difficulty of the other
response times reveal the two decisions do not form simultaneously
both dimensions are acquired in parallel for the initial ~0.1 s
after this period evidence is incorporated serially in time-multiplexed bouts
interpretations: a central bottleneck precludes updating more than one decision at a time
a sensory buffer stores evidence while decision access is blocked, plausibly explaining long cognitive timescales
